title: Recovery After Stroke 404: QEEG-guided laser story
why: Interesting story and vocabulary source; dramatic acquired-prosopagnosia claim is anecdotal and not enough to alter protocol.
next_action: Keep as curiosity; do not promote as evidence.

title: tPBM & qEEG: Guided Photobiomodulation Protocol for Stroke Rehabilitation
why: Technical case-study presentation; directly matches QEEG-guided PBM idea but appears non-independent and case-level.
next_action: Use only to extract questions: EEG montage, artifact control, targeting algorithm, dose, adverse events, outcomes.

title: Interview with Dr Margaret Naeser on brain photobiomodulation
why: Named researcher lead; Naeser has real publications in TBI and chronic stroke aphasia, but not direct hemianopia restoration evidence.
next_action: Keep Naeser papers in watchlist; separate aphasia/TBI/cognition from Eric's visual-field target.

title: ACNS Guidelines and Consensus Statements: Quantitative EEG
why: Official neurophysiology guideline source; QEEG is adjunctive analysis, not an oracle or standalone diagnosis.
next_action: Use as the boundary source for any QEEG discussion.

title: Use of Quantitative EEG for Diagnosis of Mild Traumatic Brain Injury
why: ACNS guideline notes persistent controversy and emphasizes qualified EEG acquisition/review.
next_action: Use to require raw EEG review, artifact control, and qualified interpretation.

title: Potential and Challenges of Transcranial Photobiomodulation for the Treatment of Stroke
why: Good stroke-specific review: mechanisms plausible, translation challenges remain.
next_action: Use as the main balanced tPBM stroke review.

title: Application of non-invasive transcranial photobiomodulation in ischemic stroke
why: Fresh 2026 review of ischemic-stroke mechanisms and clinical insights.
next_action: Add to monthly watchlist; review once full text is processed.

title: Transcranial Near-infrared Laser Therapy in Chronic Ischemic Stroke
source: Journal of the Saudi Heart Association 2026
why: Randomized sham-controlled chronic ischemic stroke trial found no significant between-group NIHSS benefit.
next_action: Use as key caution against assuming clinical benefit.

title: Red-light photobiomodulation in post-stroke cognitive impairment
why: Positive randomized trial for post-stroke cognition/neuropsychiatric symptoms, not hemianopia.
next_action: Track as adjacent stroke-cognition evidence, not visual-field evidence.

title: Red/NIR tPBM in chronic stroke aphasia
why: Naeser case series with improved naming and fMRI connectivity changes in chronic aphasia; small, uncontrolled, not HH.
next_action: Use as Naeser anchor but keep category separate.

title: 1064-nm tPBM improves cerebral metabolism measures in vivo
why: Human physiology evidence that tPBM can change oxygenated hemoglobin and oxidized cytochrome c oxidase measures.
next_action: Use only as mechanism/plausibility support, not rehab outcome proof.

title: Effect of PBM on electrical activity of the brain in stroke patients
why: Not-yet-recruiting stroke aphasia trial measuring PBM effects on brain electrical activity.
next_action: Monitor for EEG/PBM methods and outcomes.

title: QEEG photobiomodulation stroke query
why: Useful for finding names and links, but no claims accepted from posts.
next_action: Run monthly; save only DOI, PMID, trial, institution, or guideline links.
